items.message: Welcome to IBM Future Now! Your onboarding journey starts today. Check your checklist for Day 1 tasks.
All new starters must complete Security Awareness training by end of Week 1.

tasks.label: Set up IBM w3id account and MFA
Read and acknowledge IBM Code of Conduct
Join team standup and introduce yourself
Set up IBM Slack and join team channels
Submit bank details to HR via Workday
Request access to IBM GitHub Enterprise

courses.title: Security Awareness
courses.description: IBM mandatory annual security awareness training. Covers phishing awareness, data handling, password security, and IBM security policies. Must be completed within 5 business days of your start date.

courses.title: Code of Conduct
courses.description: IBM Business Conduct Guidelines covering ethical standards, conflicts of interest, and IBM values. Mandatory for all new starters.

courses.title: AI Fundamentals
courses.description: An introduction to AI concepts, IBM watsonx, and responsible AI principles. Recommended for all consultants joining IBM Future Now.

courses.title: GDPR & Data Privacy
courses.description: Covers GDPR obligations, IBM data classification, client data handling, and how to report a data breach. Mandatory for all UK employees.

courses.title: IBM+
courses.description: IBM+ is your personalised benefits and perks portal. This module walks you through your full benefits package including pension, health, and employee discounts.

courses.title: Role-Specific Learning Path
courses.description: Your personalised learning path for the Consultant, Digital Strategy role. Covers IBM Garage methodology, client delivery frameworks, and key tools.

links.label: Submit My Timesheet
links.description: Log hours, project codes and weekly time entries

links.label: Claim Expenses
links.description: Submit receipts, travel and business expenses

links.label: Book Annual Leave
links.description: Request holiday, check your entitlement and balance

links.label: Raise an IT Ticket
links.description: Report hardware issues, request software or access

links.label: View My Benefits
links.description: Health, pension, perks and employee assistance

links.label: View Payslip
links.description: Access your monthly payslips and pay history

links.label: IBM Learning Portal
links.description: Courses, certifications and your learning history

links.label: Flexible & Remote Working
links.description: Hybrid policy, office days and remote work guidance

links.label: Performance & Goals
links.description: Set your objectives and track performance reviews

links.label: Wellbeing & Support
links.description: Mental health resources, EAP and wellbeing support

links.label: IBM Org Chart
links.description: Find colleagues, teams and reporting lines

links.label: Data Privacy Policy
links.description: IBM's GDPR and data handling responsibilities

phases.label: First 30 Days
phases.subtitle: LEARN
goals: Understand your team's mission and current projects
Complete all mandatory IBM training modules
Meet every member of your immediate team
Set up all tools and system access

phases.label: Days 31–60
phases.subtitle: CONTRIBUTE
goals: Take ownership of a defined workstream or task
Build relationships beyond your immediate team
Attend IBM Future Now all-hands or community event
Share early learnings with your manager

phases.label: Days 61–90
phases.subtitle: LEAD
goals: Own a project or deliverable end-to-end
Propose one improvement to a team process
Conduct your 90-day review with your manager
Begin mentoring or supporting the next new starter

config.title: Key Contacts & Tools to Set Up

contacts.name: Sarah Chen
contacts.description: Performance, objectives, project allocation, career development, and escalations. Sarah is your primary contact for all things work-related.

contacts.name: Priya Patel
contacts.description: Day-to-day questions, team culture, how things work in practice, and unwritten rules. Priya is your onboarding buddy and first port of call.

contacts.name: HR Business Partner
contacts.description: Policies, pay & benefits, Workday, and any HR queries. Raise a ticket via the HR portal or message directly.

contacts.name: IT Support
contacts.description: Hardware issues, access requests, VPN, software installs, and IBM ID problems. Raise a ticket via the IT helpdesk.

documents.title: Expenses Policy
documents.description: What you can claim, limits, how to submit

documents.title: Annual Leave Policy
documents.description: Entitlement, how to book, approval process
documents.askBobPrompt: How do I book annual leave?

documents.title: Flexible Working Guide
documents.description: Remote work, hybrid expectations, office days
documents.askBobPrompt: What is the hybrid working policy?

documents.title: Learning & Development
documents.description: Training budget, IBM certifications, career paths
documents.askBobPrompt: What training is available to me?

documents.title: IT Support
documents.description: Hardware issues, access requests, software installs
documents.askBobPrompt: How do I raise an IT support ticket?

documents.title: Benefits & Wellbeing
documents.description: Health, pension, perks and employee assistance
documents.askBobPrompt: What benefits am I entitled to?

documents.title: Data Privacy Policy
documents.description: How IBM handles data, GDPR responsibilities

communities.name: IBM AI & Automation Community
communities.description: Cross-IBM practitioners working on AI, ML, and automation. Monthly deep-dives, demos, and a highly active Slack channel.
communities.bobNote: This community is a great fit for your role at IBM Future Now. The next meetup is at Southbank Hub — you are already invited via your calendar.

communities.name: IBM Future Now Consultants Network
communities.description: Peer knowledge-sharing, project case studies, and a dedicated new joiners channel. Your closest professional community.
communities.bobNote: Sarah Chen and your team colleagues are active members. A great place to ask questions and share learnings freely.

communities.name: Early Careers & New Joiners Network
communities.description: For people in their first 12 months at IBM. Social events, speed networking, and a buddy matching programme.
communities.memberCount: Open to all in first 12 months
communities.bobNote: Specifically designed for new starters like you. Virtual coffee every Thursday at 16:00 — a great way to meet people across divisions.

communities.name: IBM Women in Technology UK
communities.description: Supports gender diversity and inclusion across IBM UK. Regular events, mentorship, and an active sponsorship programme.
communities.bobNote: Open to everyone — all are actively encouraged to participate regardless of gender identity.

communities.name: IBM Sustainability & ESG Network
communities.description: IBMers focused on sustainability, climate, and responsible business. Growing fast alongside IBM's sustainability practice.
communities.bobNote: Relevant to IBM's growing sustainability consulting practice. This community shares client case studies, research, and emerging ESG frameworks.
